verbatim_text: So it's a 350 million parameter model. And for those of you that work with language models, you might know that this is a tiny model. Anything that's less than tens of billions of parameters is considered quite small. And this is less than half of a billion. This is a very, very small model. And it was trained on 28 trillion tokens. That's a lot of tokens. And so people were going on the internet saying, rest in peace, Chinchilla. That Chinchilla scaling is dead. Standard notions of how we should design our pre-training recipes. These have gone out the window. Okay.
